---
name: iwsdk-dev
description: >
  MUST read this skill BEFORE developing VR/MR/browser-first 3D applications
  using IWSDK (Immersive Web SDK). Covers project scaffolding, headless
  browser setup, CLI tools, reference system, ECS debugging, XR emulation,
  verification workflows, deployment, and known issues.
---

# IWSDK Development Guide

**Current version: 0.4.1** · [iwsdk.dev](https://iwsdk.dev) · [GitHub](https://github.com/facebook/immersive-web-sdk)

This guide is for AI agents and developers building WebXR or browser-first 3D
applications with IWSDK in cloud/headless Linux environments (no physical GPU,
no display server). It assumes Node.js satisfies IWSDK's package engine range:
`>=20.19.0 <21.0.0-0 || >=22.12.0 <23.0.0-0 || >=24.0.0`.
The managed dev browser uses Playwright Chromium; the dev plugin installs the
matching Chromium binary when it is missing.

### CLI-First Operating Model

This guide is intentionally **CLI-first** for cloud-based harnesses. Treat
`npx iwsdk ...` commands as the primary control surface for setup, runtime
inspection, screenshots, ECS debugging, XR emulation, and reference queries.

Reason: MCP adapter support in cloud harnesses is often missing, stale, or
partially wired. Relying on MCP tool availability can degrade generation quality
because the agent may plan around tools that are not actually connected. Use MCP
tools only when the harness clearly exposes them and they are already working;
otherwise use the CLI commands in this guide. Do not rewrite CLI examples into
MCP-only workflows.

---

## 1. Project Scaffolding

Always use the official `@iwsdk/create` package. Use `--yes` to bypass
interactive prompts.

**Do not hardcode scaffolding flags.** Infer the correct flags from the
request. When ambiguous, ask.

### Hard Constraints (Always Apply)

- **`--no-metaspatial`** — use the manual workflow in cloud/headless Linux.
  Meta Spatial Editor authoring requires the macOS/Windows GUI; the Linux CLI is
  for CI/CD build workflows, not GUI authoring.

### Step 1: XR vs Browser-First

| Target | Flag | When to use |
|--------|------|-------------|
| **XR** | `--xr` (default) | VR or AR headset experiences. Proceed to Step 2. |
| **Browser-first** | `--no-xr` | Desktop/mobile browser 3D app. No headset required. The scaffold sets `xr: false`, but `@iwsdk/create` does **not** currently wire browser locomotion, mouselook, or browser grabbing for you; add `features.locomotion: { browserControls: true }` and app-owned camera controls manually when needed. |

### Step 2: XR Mode (XR only)

| Mode | Flag | When to use |
|------|------|-------------|
| **VR** | `--mode vr` | Fully immersive virtual environment. |
| **AR** | `--mode ar` | Augmented / mixed reality with passthrough. |

### Step 3: Features

| Feature | Flag | Enable when… | Disable when… |
|---------|------|-------------|---------------|
| **Physics** | `--physics` / `--no-physics` | Gravity, collisions, bouncing, throwing. | UI-only, data viz, grid-snapping board games. |
| **Locomotion** | `--locomotion` / `--no-locomotion` | VR user moves through a large space (teleport/slide). For browser-first, configure `features.locomotion: { browserControls: true }` manually after scaffolding. | Stationary experience. |
| **Grabbing** | `--grabbing` / `--no-grabbing` | XR users pick up, move, or manipulate objects. With `--no-xr`, the create flag is ignored; add browser interaction behavior manually. | Gaze/pointer-only or observational. |
| **Scene Understanding** | `--scene-understanding` / `--no-scene-understanding` | AR: interact with real-world surfaces. | AR without surface interaction. |
| **Environment Raycast** | `--environment-raycast` / `--no-environment-raycast` | AR: cast rays against real-world geometry. | No real-world hit testing needed. |

### Step 4: Source (optional)

| Flag | Effect |
|------|--------|
| *(none)* | Default npm source selected by the current `@iwsdk/create` package and starter-assets recipe. |
| `--canary` | Baked-in CloudFront CDN canary bundle. |
| `--canary <url>` | Custom bundle URL for internal builds. |

### Examples

```bash
# VR game (stationary, physics for gameplay)
npx @iwsdk/create@0.4.1 space-pong --yes --mode vr --physics --no-locomotion --grabbing --no-metaspatial

# AR object placer
npx @iwsdk/create@0.4.1 ar-placer --yes --mode ar --physics --scene-understanding --no-metaspatial

# Browser-first 3D game (no headset). Add browser locomotion/camera controls in code.
npx @iwsdk/create@0.4.1 browser-game --yes --no-xr --physics --no-metaspatial
```

---

{"content":"# Holo Golf VR — Round 5 Build Journal
**Date:** 2026-05-21
**Start:** 22:55 UTC | **End:** 23:07 UTC
**Duration:** ~40 minutes (including planning and assessment)
**Files:** 27 → 29 | **Lines:** 7,147 → 8,067 (+920 lines)

## Assessment
- Clean TypeScript compile (0 errors)
- All 27 holes, 3 courses, VR+browser dual runtime functional
- Previous 4 rounds built solid foundation: physics, audio, effects, XR input, leaderboard

## Improvements Made

### New Obstacle Types
1. **Gravity Well** (`gravitywell.ts`)
   - Circular attractor that pulls ball toward center
   - Animated concentric rings (pulsing inward), floating core orb, orbiting torus
   - Force scales inversely with distance from center
   - Point light + eerie warping SFX
   - Added to Course 2 Hole 9, Course 3 Holes 5 and 9

2. **Speed Boost Pad** (`gravitywell.ts`)
   - Directional pad that accelerates ball on contact
   - Animated chevrons flowing in boost direction
   - Edge wireframe + point light
   - Cooldown prevents repeated triggers
   - Added to Course 2 Hole 9, Course 3 Holes 7 and 9

### Visual Polish (postfx.ts)
3. **Scanline/CRT Overlay**
   - Subtle horizontal scanlines (every 4px) + faint vertical grid
   - Radial vignette darkening edges
   - `mix-blend-mode: overlay` at 6% opacity for minimal intrusiveness
   - Reinforces holodeck aesthetic without hurting readability

4. **Camera Shake System**
   - Triggered on: wall bounces, bumper hits, OOB, water hazard, hole-in-one
   - Intensity proportional to impact force
   - Exponential decay for natural feel
   - Applied as position offset to orbit camera

5. **Slow-Motion Effect**
   - Triggers when ball is <0.3m from hole and moving slowly (dramatic near-miss moments)
   - Time scale smoothly lerps to 0.4x, then returns to 1.0x
   - Applied to all game systems (physics, effects, obstacles) but NOT input/UI

6. **Ball Spin Animation**
   - Mesh rotation proportional to velocity direction and speed
   - X-axis rotation from Z-velocity, Z-axis rotation from X-velocity
   - Makes the ball feel physically grounded

7. **Enhanced Ball Glow**
   - Glow intensity increases with ball speed
   - Light color shifts to brighter cyan at high speeds
   - Creates satisfying visual feedback loop for powerful shots

### Audio Improvements
8. **Rolling Ball Sound**
   - Brownian noise through bandpass filter
   - Volume and center frequency scale with ball speed
   - Smooth transitions (no pops/clicks)

9. **Distinct Score SFX**
   - Eagle: dramatic ascending 6-note arpeggio + double sparkle
   - Birdie: bright chirpy ascending pattern
   - Par: clean satisfying two-tone confirmation
   - Bogey: slightly disappointed descending tone

10. **New Obstacle SFX**
    - Gravity well: eerie descending warping tone
    - Speed boost: ascending filtered noise whoosh + tone cascade
    - Streak chime: escalating tones based on streak length

### Game Feel
11. **Streak/Combo System**
    - Tracks consecutive under-par holes within a course
    - Shows toast notification at 3+ streak ("HOT STREAK! ×3")
    - Escalates: "ON FIRE! ×5", "UNSTOPPABLE! ×7", "PERFECT ROUND!"
    - Accompanied by escalating chime SFX
    - Resets on course start or over-par hole

## Technical Notes
- All new features integrate cleanly with existing XR controller system
- Gravity well force is additive with wind zones
- Speed boost goes through same zone-checking pipeline as teleporters/wind/ice
- Camera shake offset applied after camera position lerp, before lookAt
- Slow motion scales game dt but NOT input dt (preserves controller responsiveness)
- Build requires Node 20+ (nvm use 20) due to Vite 7.3 crypto.hash requirement

## Deployment
- Commit: `0397944` on master
- Deployed to gh-pages via direct branch push
- Live at: https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/

## Round 5 Summary

**Holo Golf VR — Round 5 complete.** 29 source files, 8,067 lines, 220 total minutes across 5 rounds.

### What was added (+920 lines):

**New Obstacle Types:**
- **Gravity Well** — Circular attractor pulling ball toward center with animated pulsing rings, floating core orb, orbiting torus wireframe, and point light. Force scales inversely with distance. Added to Course 2 Hole 9 and Course 3 Holes 5 & 9.
- **Speed Boost Pad** — Directional acceleration pad with animated chevrons flowing in boost direction. Cooldown prevents repeated triggers. Added to Course 2 Hole 9 and Course 3 Holes 7 & 9.

**Visual Polish:**
- **Scanline/CRT overlay** — Subtle horizontal scanlines + faint vertical grid + radial vignette at 6% opacity. Reinforces holodeck aesthetic.
- **Camera shake** — Triggers on wall/bumper bounces, OOB, water hazard, hole-in-one. Intensity proportional to impact. Exponential decay.
- **Slow-motion** — Dramatic 0.4x slowdown when ball approaches hole cup (<0.3m, slow speed). Applied to game systems but not input.
- **Ball spin animation** — Rotation proportional to velocity direction.
- **Enhanced ball glow** — Intensity and color shift based on speed.

**Audio (7 new sounds):**
- Rolling ball procedural sound (Brownian noise, speed-reactive)
- Distinct score SFX: eagle, birdie, par, bogey (each unique)
- Gravity well pull SFX, speed boost whoosh SFX, streak chime SFX

**Game Feel:**
- **Streak/combo system** — Tracks consecutive under-par holes. Toasts at 3+ ("HOT STREAK!"), escalating up to "PERFECT ROUND!" with matching chime.

**Deployment:** Pushed to `master` and deployed to gh-pages at https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/
